name: Coveo
what_it_does: AI-powered enterprise search and relevance platform. Coveo connects to enterprise content sources (Salesforce, Confluence, SharePoint, ServiceNow, etc.) and provides a unified search experience with ML-based relevance ranking, personalization, and generative AI answers. Used for self-service customer portals, employee knowledge bases, and e-commerce product search. Provides REST API for embedding search in applications and programmatic index management.
use_cases: Build AI customer service agents that search across enterprise knowledge bases (Salesforce cases, Confluence, SharePoint) via Coveo's unified search API
Create intelligent internal knowledge assistants that search employee documentation with ML-relevance ranking and generative AI answers
Power e-commerce search with personalized product results using Coveo's ML ranking and click-through learning
Index proprietary content sources via Coveo's Push API for RAG agent knowledge retrieval
Integrate Coveo's generative answering feature into agent workflows for citation-backed answers from enterprise knowledge
not_for: Small teams or startups — Coveo is enterprise-priced with significant implementation overhead
Simple full-text search — Elasticsearch, Typesense, or Meilisearch are simpler and cheaper for basic search needs
Real-time web search — Coveo indexes internal enterprise content, not the live web
best_when: Enterprise organizations with large, distributed knowledge bases across multiple systems (Salesforce, Confluence, ServiceNow) who need unified search with ML relevance and generative AI answers.
avoid_when: Your content lives in one or two systems and basic keyword/BM25 search suffices — Coveo's AI features and price aren't justified for simple search needs.

auth.notes: API key or OAuth2 token for REST API access. Separate keys for search (query API), indexing (push API), and administration. Keys generated in Coveo Administration Console. Role-based access control.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier. Trial with guided setup.
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise custom pricing based on content volume, users, and features; typically $40K-200K+/year
pricing.notes: Enterprise-only pricing requires sales engagement. Implementation services often included in enterprise contracts. Pricing depends heavily on content sources and user count.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits by plan; search API is optimized for high-throughput querying

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns structured JSON with error codes. Push API returns async ingestion job IDs for status tracking. Search errors clearly identify permission and query issues.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Search queries are idempotent (read-only). Push API document indexing is idempotent by document ID — same document ID overwrites previous version. Analytics events are not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Coveo's query language (Coveo Query Language - CQL) is different from standard SQL or Lucene — agents must learn CQL syntax for complex queries
Source connectors require Coveo's crawling infrastructure to be set up before content is searchable — agents can't create new content sources via API
Push API indexing is async — pushed documents may take seconds to minutes to appear in search results depending on indexing queue
Generative answering (Coveo GenAI) requires separate configuration and additional licensing — not available by default
Analytics tracking is required for ML models to improve — agents must implement usage analytics calls for relevance learning to function
Enterprise sales process required before any technical evaluation — no self-service trial

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II, GDPR, HIPAA, FedRAMP authorized. Role-based access control with fine-grained search permissions. Data residency options globally. SAML/SSO for enterprise. Content security enforced at search time via user context.
